channel.title: State from source and state for frontend
channel.description: Hi, I have a question about states. Is there anyway to implement the typical react hook state like "useState" for front end purposes for a specific component, like you would on a "normal" react app? (e.g. presenting components or storing input values etc)?

item.title: State from source and state for frontend
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
Hi @albin
Glad you managed to make it work

albin:

Is there anyway to implement the typical react hook state like “useState” for front end purposes for a specific component, like you would on a “normal” react app?

Yes, of course. In a frontity app you will have:

  • A “global” state that is also grouped in different namespaces → the “theme” namespace is the place to work with variables you want to use across different components
  • A “local” state (component scope) that can be implemented with hooks like useState → these variables are the right option when the data doesn’t need to be shared w/ any other (parent or sibling) component

Read full topic
item.source: State from source and state for frontend

item.title: State from source and state for frontend
item.dc:creator: albin
item.description:
Never mind it works now!

Read full topic

item.description:
Hi, I have a question about states. Is there anyway to implement the typical react hook state like “useState” for front end purposes for a specific component, like you would on a “normal” react app? (e.g. presenting components or storing input values etc)?

Read full topic
